free_text: 25 keV-neutron capture cross-sections
Preliminary data given
Reactor CIRUS
Foils, metallic powders or powders oxides
A well shielded 3 inch X 3 inch NaI(Tl) crystal
Activation in the same geometry using the keV
and thermal fluxes.
Iron-filtered reactor neutron beam at 25 keV
Energy spread of 1.8 keV for the 25 keV neutrons
Normalized to the thermal cross section of the same
sample
Thermal value
25 keV value
127I(n,g)128I cross section at 25 keV
Corrected for self-shielding of neutrons.
Total error given as:
Statistical error in the area under
photopeak                        (<2%)
Error in time measurements       (<1%)
Compiler's Note:
It might be pertinent to use EN-MAX and EN-MIN with
(NG,SPA) instead of EN and EN-RSL. However, the entry
follows after the author's description literally.
== 81/05/29 compiler.
The decision to supersede the data published at the
first reference by data from third ref was taken by
compiler (VP) and based on the principle that latest
publication  must contain the final results.

By compiler = The author put this reaction as the
total. Because transition from the metastable state
(T1/2 = 48 sec) to the ground state (T1/2 = 82 sec)
beta decay is isomeric transition and measurement
probably was started long enough after the isomeric
transition.
